summary: Tested split-suggestion endpoint + Cleanup Copilot Suggest-Split flow (iter29). Backend 3/3 PASS. Frontend UI/rendering PASS (all testids present, labels correct, Apply/Ignore work, unimodal Walmart correctly gets no hint). Two integration bugs found: (1) auto-submit after Apply doesn't fire send() — accepted per request; manual send-btn click works. (2) CRITICAL: interceptor mis-parses SplitHintForm's templated text so 'above $100 is Office Supplies' yields category name '$100 Is Office Supplies' instead of 'Office Supplies'. 6 of 12 SplitDemo txns were categorized under the wrong (malformed) category name.

ui_bugs.issue: Auto-submit via setTimeout(send,30) does NOT fire — input is populated but send is not dispatched. Manual click on ai-chat-send works. Root cause likely: `send` closure captures stale `input` value; needs to read via inputRef or trigger via handleSubmit path.

SplitHintForm emits `under $100 is Meals, above $100 is Office Supplies`. The regex on line 1062 matches but the 3rd capture group greedily consumes the '$100 is ' prefix into the aboveCat, producing category name '$100 Is Office Supplies'. Verified in DB: 6 txns saved under literal category '$100 Is Office Supplies'. Fix: either (a) change SplitHintForm templated string to `under $${t} is X, above is Y` (matches existing parser), OR (b) extend regex to `(?:above|over|...)\s+(?:\$?[\d,.km]+\s+)?(?:is|are|→|=)?\s*(.+?)`.

integration_issues.issue: End-to-end works after manual send click. Backend correctly created 2 rules (source=user_multi_bulk_approve) and updated 12 txns, but one category name is malformed due to interceptor parsing bug.

action_items: FIX (critical): Adjust range-split parsing so `above $100 is X` is parsed as X, not '$100 Is X'. Recommended: change SplitHintForm templated string on AiPanel.jsx:1915 from `under $${t} is ${belowCat}, above $${t} is ${aboveCat}` to `under $${t} is ${belowCat}, above is ${aboveCat}` (matches existing parser).

FIX (medium): Auto-submit after Apply doesn't fire — setTimeout(send,30) captures stale input closure. Consider using inputRef inside send() or invoking submit handler with the templated string directly.
critical_code_review_comments: AiPanel.jsx is now 2496 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. Extract SplitHintForm, BulkApproveCard, MultiCard, and the cleanup-inquiry interceptor into separate modules.

Range-split regex (line 1062) is untested for the exact string SplitHintForm produces — this is the root cause of the category-name bug. Add a unit test for the parser with input `under $100 is Meals, above $100 is Office Supplies`.
seed_data_creation: None. SplitDemo Vendor (contact 72da725e-970b-463d-b22e-b169ff3352d2) already seeded. After testing, DB state was RESET: all 12 SplitDemo txns set to human_reviewed=False, posted=False, needs_review=True, category_account_*=None, ai_confidence=0.5. All 2 rules with source='user_multi_bulk_approve' were deleted. Synthetic contact and txns preserved.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoint /api/companies/{cid}/transactions/split-suggestion works correctly (bimodal + unimodal + auth). Frontend SplitHintForm renders correctly and all testids exist. Two bugs remain in AiPanel.jsx: (1) auto-submit closure, (2) range-split regex greedily consumes threshold prefix on the 'above' side. When retesting after fix, verify the DB after clicking Apply → Yes: `db.transactions.find({contact_id:'72da725e-970b-463d-b22e-b169ff3352d2'})` should show 6 rows with category_account_name='Meals' and 6 with category_account_name='Office Supplies' (NOT '$100 Is Office Supplies').
rca_of_the_issue: The SplitHintForm.onApply handler at AiPanel.jsx:1915 emits `under $${t} is ${below}, above $${t} is ${above}` and hands off to the range-split regex at AiPanel.jsx:1062. The regex's third capture group `(.+?)` is preceded by `(?:is|are|goes?\s+to|→|=)?` — this is OPTIONAL, so when the input contains a second `$100 is` after 'above ', the optional group doesn't consume it and `(.+?)` non-greedily matches `$100 is Office Supplies` (anchored to end). Result: category name literal is '$100 is Office Supplies' (title-cased by cleanCat → '$100 Is Office Supplies'). Two fixes possible; the SplitHintForm-side fix is safest (single line change).
